name: kit-page
description: The canonical Filament PANEL SHELL, rendered from a lievit-kit KitPageView/KitPageComponent onto the lievit-ui shell primitives (sidebar + sidebar/group + sidebar/item, breadcrumb, dropdown-menu, avatar, theme-switcher, notification-bell, input-group, popover, icon, button, badge, field, input, alert, section, infolist-entry, link). Ships the Filament panel chrome server-first (every control a real <a href> / <form> / native popover, strict-CSP clean, no inline script): the app-shell layout (skip-link + sidebar column + main column), the sidebar navigation tree (groups + items + badges + active aria-current), the sticky topbar (brand spacer + global search field + grouped-results popover + notification bell + user menu), the page header (breadcrumbs + h1 + subheading + header-actions cluster), the user menu (avatar dropdown + profile header + theme switcher + items + logout), and the four resource page bodies (list table / create-edit form / view infolist) plus the standalone auth pages (login / register / password-reset / email-verification). This is the kit's REFERENCE panel render: the binding tier between the kit view-models (NavigationBuilder / AdminListView / AdminFormView / AdminViewView / AuthFormView / GlobalSearchResults) and the lievit-ui partials, so an adopter gets a working Filament-shaped admin without hand-rolling the chrome. Composes blocks/app-shell's pattern but adds the notification-bell topbar slot + the theme-switcher in the user menu the bare block does not expose. Mirrors the kit-table reference render.
docs: Copies the kit panel shell to your JTE root under kit/: kit/page.jte (+ kit/page/{sidebar-nav,page-header,user-menu,global-search,resource-list,resource-form,resource-view,infolist-grid,auth}.jte), reached as @template.kit.page(...). It renders a KitPageView (the bundle io.lievit.kit.page.KitPageComponent produces from a Panel): pass `page` (the KitPageView carrying brand / nav tree / breadcrumb / user + menu / theme / search / notification facts) and a `content` body slot; optionally `headerActions`, `searchResults` (a GlobalSearchResults for the grouped-results popover) and a `notifications` panel slot. The four resource wrappers drop a page BODY into the shell: kit/page/resource-list (wraps kit/table), resource-form (binds an AdminFormView), resource-view (binds an AdminViewView); kit/page/auth renders the standalone auth pages from an AuthFormView. KitPageComponent.navGroups projects the panel's NavigationBuilder (visibility + sort honoured) into the template's nav-row idiom, resolving each nav Icon alias to a Lucide glyph via the IconRegistry; userMenu(logoutAction) assembles the profile + configured items + logout rows. With no chrome facts the shell degrades cleanly (no user => no user menu, no search action => no search field, notificationCount<0 => no bell, no theme => no switcher), so a host can start minimal. UNLIKE the lievit-ui blocks/app-shell (the bare sidebar+search+user-menu composition), this kit item is the FILAMENT-faithful superset wired to the kit panel view-models: it adds the notification-bell topbar slot + the theme-switcher in the user menu. It path-depends on the io.github.lievit:lievit-kit jar (the KitPageView/KitPageComponent/AdminFormView/AdminViewView/AuthFormView/GlobalSearchResults view-models the templates import). The source is yours: edit freely (Filament's publish-views model, the engine in the jar, the views overridable).
